name: How to Add Your Business Listing in eLocal?
description: Learn how to add your business to eLocal with this easy-to-follow guide from Synup. Get your business seen by more potential customers and improve your online visibility today.
main_title: How to Add Your Business Listing in eLocal?
richtext:
As a business owner, constantly seeking opportunities to augment your brand's exposure and ensnare more customers is crucial. A useful tactic to accomplish this objective involves listing your enterprise on online directories, and amongst the most prominent ones is eLocal. This comprehensive guide will elucidate the intricate steps involved in adding your business listing to eLocal, thereby enabling you to showcase your enterprise to a wider pool of prospective customers.

Why Add Your Business Listing to eLocal?

Before we dive into the granular details of adding your business to eLocal, it is essential to first understand why it is a necessary undertaking. The answer is simple: more visibility. eLocal is a popular online directory that receives a significant amount of traffic from users searching for businesses in their area. Listing your business on eLocal elevates your chances of being discovered by potential customers, ultimately leading to increased sales and revenue for your enterprise.

Step-by-Step Guide to Add Your Business Listing in eLocal

Step 1: Go to the eLocal website (www.elocal.com) 

Step 2: Once there, click on the ‘Join Our Pro Network’ list and Select ‘Free Listing’ from the top right corner of the homepage.

Step 3: Next is to enter your business information and click ‘Check For My Business’ is already listed with eLocal.com or not.

Step 4: If not listed, you will be needed to fill out the registration form with your business information, including your name, address, phone number, and email address.

Step 5: Select your business address viability to all visitors or only your locality visitors. And click ‘Sign Up Now’.

Step 6: Choose your business category and subcategory, and provide a brief description of your business. eLocal has a wide range of categories to choose from, so take your time to select the one that accurately describes your business.

Step 7: Select your services offered on each section by a tick and click ‘Next’

Step 8: Finally, verify your email address.

Step 9: Review and confirm your listing details, and then submit your listing.

After you've submitted your listing, eLocal will review it before publishing it on their website. Rest assured, you'll receive an email alert once your listing is given the green light and is live on the platform. To ensnare potential customers, it's crucial to maintain accurate and up-to-date business information.

How to Claim an Existing eLocal Business Listing

If your business is already listed on eLocal, there’s no need to create a new listing—just claim it. Visit www.elocal.com and use the search function to find your business. During signup, when you click Check For My Business (Step 3 in the signup flow above), eLocal will show if your business already exists in their directory. If it does, you can claim it, or call eLocal directly for assistance. A representative will guide you through the process.

To claim your listing, eLocal requires verification. Be prepared to provide documents such as your business license, tax records, business email verification, and phone and address confirmation. Once verified, you gain full access.

After your claim is approved, log in to your dashboard to update outdated information. Upload your logo, add authentic photos, and refresh services.

If you encounter duplicate listings, don’t worry. Email eLocal support to have them merged. Duplicate listings can confuse customers and hurt your local SEO, so it’s important to resolve them promptly.

‍

Tips for Optimizing Your eLocal Business Listing

Once your business is listed on eLocal, optimization is key to maximizing visibility, attracting quality leads, and converting searchers into customers. Here are some tips to optimize your eLocal profile:

  1. Complete every section of your profile
  2. Maintain consistent NAP (name, address, phone) information
  3. Choose the right business categories
  4. Write a business description that sells
  5. Add high-quality photos and your business logo
  6. Add business slogans or taglines
  7. Set your service area accurately
  8. Select address visibility options carefully
  9. Keep your listing information updated
  10. Monitor your performance
  11. Respond quickly to inquiries
  12. Understand eLocal's pay-per-call model (if you upgrade)

Read More: Local Listing Management Guide to Improve Your Ranking

‍

Conclusion

eLocal is a powerful directory that can boost your business’s visibility, showcasing your NAP, business description, website, services, and payment options. Its pay-per-call program can drive targeted leads straight to your phone.

But let’s be clear. eLocal alone will not dominate local search. It is just one piece of a larger local SEO puzzle. Manually managing listings across eLocal, Google Business Profile, Yelp, and dozens of other directories invites inconsistencies, and every small error chips away at your rankings.

Tools like Synup remove that risk entirely. By centralizing your listings across 100+ directories, auto-syncing your NAP, and keeping every profile accurate, Synup ensures your business stays visible, credible, and competitive while saving you hours of tedious work.
